pr.title: make CLIFFCATCH_O/U macros safe and define user_data struct of HSD_GObj class 7
pr.author: VetriTheRetri

summary: PR #515 made two small but reusable header/type improvements and did a broad formatting cleanup in item2.c. It parenthesized the CLIFFCATCH_O and CLIFFCATCH_U macro arguments so callers with expressions are safer, renamed Ness yo-yo collision flag usage from the vague COLL_AIR to MPCOLL_GRPUSH while adding related MPCOLL_* masks, added GObj class constants for stage and item-link objects, and introduced an ItemLink struct for HSD_GObj class 7 user_data. The large item2.c diff appears mostly style/include-order/cast/brace cleanup rather than semantic decomp progress, based on the excerpt.
what_changed: In fighter.h, CLIFFCATCH_O(fp) and CLIFFCATCH_U(fp) now wrap the macro parameter use as ((fp)->x2C_facing_direction ...), avoiding precedence hazards when the argument is not a simple identifier.
In common_structs.h, COLL_AIR was replaced with MPCOLL_GRPUSH at value 0x8000, and additional collision masks MPCOLL_LEFTWALL 0xFC0, MPCOLL_RIGHTWALL 0x3F, and MPCOLL_CEIL 0x6000 were added.
In ftNess_AttackHi4.c, Ness yo-yo environment collision checks were updated to test MPCOLL_GRPUSH instead of COLL_AIR.
In baselib gobj.h, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_STAGE 0x3 and HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEMLINK 0x7 were added; ITEMLINK is commented as used by chain-type items in-game to link multiple parts together.
In item.h, a new ItemLink struct was added as the user_data struct of GObj class 7, with prev/next links, velocity/position/vector fields, bit flags, CollData, and GObj pointers at x1D0 and x1D4.

In item2.c, many lines were reformatted: include order changed, braces moved to one-line style, casts spaced as `(f32) expr`, switch indentation normalized, comments trimmed, and an inline ASM block for func_8026B924 was reindented. The excerpt does not show clear semantic changes there.
smart_moves: Parenthesizing macro arguments is a low-risk defensive fix: it preserves current matched intent while preventing future expression arguments from binding incorrectly with `->` or ternary operators.
Renaming COLL_AIR to MPCOLL_GRPUSH made the constant more specific to the observed collision mask behavior from Ness's Yo-Yo check rather than implying a generic airborne flag.
Adding HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEMLINK alongside an ItemLink user_data struct creates a clearer typed path for chain-type item work instead of treating class 7 objects as opaque GObjs.
Keeping class constants in sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h and user_data layout in melee/it/item.h separates engine object classification from game-specific payload layout.
The ItemLink struct preserved offset-style member names such as x0_prev, x4_next, x30_collData, x1D0_GObj, and x1D4_GObjLinkNext, which is useful when exact semantic names are not fully proven.
decomp_lessons: When a macro dereferences a pointer argument, always use `(fp)` or equivalent parentheses around the argument inside the macro body; this avoids subtle mismatches and future compiler surprises when arguments become expressions.
Prefer narrow, behavior-derived collision mask names over misleading generic labels. Here, MPCOLL_GRPUSH is better evidence-grounded than COLL_AIR for the same 0x8000 bit.
GObj class constants should be enumerated as they are discovered, especially when a new class has a distinct user_data layout. This helps future functions cast `gobj->user_data` consistently.
For partially understood structs, offset-prefixed names and cautious comments such as `Previous position?` preserve layout confidence without overstating semantics.
Large formatting-only diffs can obscure the meaningful decomp changes; future PR summaries should call out whether item2.c changes are style-only or matching-driven.
naming_conventions: Collision masks use the MPCOLL_* prefix: MPCOLL_GRPUSH, MPCOLL_LEFTWALL, MPCOLL_RIGHTWALL, MPCOLL_CEIL.
GObj class constants use HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_* with uppercase class names: HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_STAGE, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_FIGHTER, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEM, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEMLINK, HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_EFFECT.
New item-link user_data type is named ItemLink, matching the HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEMLINK class label.
Unconfirmed struct fields keep offset-based names, for example x8_vel, x14_pos, x20_vec, x30_collData, x1D0_GObj, and x1D4_GObjLinkNext.
Macro names CLIFFCATCH_O and CLIFFCATCH_U distinguish the fcmpo-based and fcmpu-based facing-direction checks, according to nearby comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The CLIFFCATCH_O and CLIFFCATCH_U macros encode ternary facing-direction choices intended to correspond to fcmpo and fcmpu comparison behavior.

item2.c retains an inline ASM version of func_8026B924 under a compiler/matching comment: `requires -g compiler flag / Frank modifications to match`; the PR excerpt only shows reindentation of that block.
_sqrtfItem uses __frsqrte followed by three Newton-Raphson refinement steps to approximate sqrtf; the PR excerpt only shows formatting/cast style changes there.
No review discussion in the provided slice explains matching motivation beyond the existing decomp.me scratch links and comments in item2.c.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The only provided human comment says the PR was rebased onto master and gives commands to reset a local repository. It is process guidance, not technical review.
No review comments were included in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEMLINK or cast class 7 user_data to ItemLink.
Search for remaining uses of COLL_AIR to determine whether MPCOLL_GRPUSH fully replaced the old name.
Audit CLIFFCATCH_O and CLIFFCATCH_U call sites for expression arguments now protected by the added parentheses.
Check whether ItemLink field comments were later refined, especially x20_vec and x1D4_GObjLinkNext.
Review item2.c history around PR #515 to distinguish pure formatting from matching-required changes.
